title: Daily Extreme (station-local calendar day high/low)
description: STORAGE SCHEMA for the Phase 3.1 daily_extremes() rollup. One row per (station, local_date) describing the calendar-day temperature extremes derived from cached hourly METAR observations. Used as the resolution source for Polymarket weather markets in non-US locales (and as a parity sanity check for US settlements). Calendar day is in the station's IANA timezone (NOT UTC). Whole-degree-C precision for international stations (round half-up to match issuer reporting); 0.1°C precision for US stations.

station.description: 4-letter ICAO identifier (e.g. "EGLL", "KNYC"). Daily extremes are computed per-ICAO; multi-airport cities (e.g. Paris LFPG vs LFPB) get separate rows that the per-event resolver picks between.

local_date.description: Calendar day in the station's IANA timezone (YYYY-MM-DD). NOT UTC — the station-local convention matches how Polymarket resolves NHIGH/NLOW-style markets internationally.

n_obs.description: Count of non-null hourly temperature observations contributing to this row. Daily aggregates with ``n_obs < 12`` are flagged ``low_coverage``: tmin/tmax/tmean are set to null and a WARNING is logged (not a hard failure — the row still ships so downstream consumers see the coverage gap explicitly).

tmin_c.description: Daily minimum temperature in Celsius. Null when ``n_obs < 12`` (low coverage). Precision: whole °C for international stations, 0.1°C for US.

tmax_c.description: Daily maximum temperature in Celsius. Null when ``n_obs < 12``. Precision matches tmin_c.

tmean_c.description: Daily mean temperature in Celsius (simple arithmetic mean across all non-null hourly samples — NOT (tmin+tmax)/2). Null when ``n_obs < 12``.

precip_inches.description: Daily accumulated precipitation in inches (sum of non-null hourly ``precip_1hr_inches``). Zero (not null) when the day had no rain but had observations.

source_tmin.description: Source identifier of the observation row that achieved the daily minimum (e.g. ``"iem"``, ``"awc"``, ``"ghcnh"``). Null when tmin_c is null.

source_tmax.description: Source identifier of the observation row that achieved the daily maximum. Null when tmax_c is null.

country.description: Informational: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the station (e.g. ``"US"``, ``"GB"``). Informational only — not used for join keys.

$comment: ARCHITECTURE DECISIONS: (1) Local-calendar-day key NOT UTC — matches how Polymarket and most international issuers resolve daily weather markets. (2) source_tmin / source_tmax preserve per-extreme provenance so a downstream consumer can tell whether a record-setting hour came from a primary METAR or a backfill PSV. (3) Coverage gate at n_obs<12 was chosen to mirror WMO's ≥1/day-as-significant rule but is intentionally permissive: half a day of obs is enough to flag a hot/cold spike but not enough to claim a reliable mean. (4) precip_inches is NOT nulled on low coverage — zero rain with two obs is still a valid ‘sum’ (it just means we only saw two zeros), and Polymarket precipitation contracts care about the total, not the sample count. (5) Whole-°C international rounding is round-half-up to match the issuer-side reporting convention (most non-US authorities publish whole °C in daily summaries); US stays at 0.1°C because that's what NWS CLI publishes.
